likely intended as `\"MemoryLeakMonitor\"`.\n- No other suspicious behavior, data handling, or obfuscation is present.\n- The source map comment indicates build artifacts, typical in production code.\n\n---\n\n### Review of Each Report\n\n#### Report 1\n- **Purpose:** Load 'MemoryLeakMointor' for leak monitoring.\n- **Anomalies:** Misspelling of 'Monitor'.\n- **Analysis:** Suspicion of obfuscation or malicious intent due to misspelling.\n- **Scores:** malware 0.2, securityRisk 0.4, confidence 0.6\n\n**Critique:**\n- The malware score (0.2) is somewhat high given the code is benign, just a module load.\n- The suspicion of malicious intent based solely on misspelling is weak; misspellings are common typos.\n- No evidence of malicious behavior.\n\n**Suggestion:**\n- Reduce malware score to 0.1.\n- Keep securityRisk at 0.4 if the module source is unverified.\n- Confidence is reasonable.\n\n---\n\n#### Report 2\n- **Purpose:** Same as above.\n- **Anomalies:** Same misspelling.\n- **Analysis:** No malicious activity, just a straightforward import.\n- **Scores:** malware 0, securityRisk 0.2, confidence 0.7\n\n**Critique:**\n- Malware score of 0 is appropriate.\n- Security risk is low; given the code's simplicity, they should be lower.\n- The security risks are generally low, mainly due to the misspelling and the potential for loading unintended modules if the name is incorrect.\n\n### My own estimates:\n\n| Metric          | Score (0-1) | Justification                                                  |\n|-----------------|-------------|----------------------------------------------------------------|\n| **Malware**    | **0.0 - 0.1** | No malicious code present; only a module load.
